title: Comms follow-up automation + edit-with-reason (lead interactions)
business_reason: Builds on FR-0004. (1) followup_policy requires that scheduling a follow-up creates a task (task_work_queue) and a reminder (notification_ops); today next_followup_at is only stored. (2) interaction_policy requires interaction edits to carry a reason with the original retained in audit; today interactions are add-only.

decisions_resolved.followup_target: Task owned by the exec queue (owner_role sales_school_outreach_executive); reminder to BOTH the lead owner and the sales manager (two notification_events: recipient_resolver lead_owner + sales_manager).
decisions_resolved.editable_fields: interaction_type, summary, outcome, next_followup_at (reason required; status -> 'edited'; original snapshot retained in audit).

param_14.question: Scheduling a follow-up on an interaction must create a follow-up task and reminders; editing an interaction must require a reason and retain the original.
param_14.answer: on_add chain (conditional on next_followup_at): ensureQueue(CRM_FOLLOWUP, role_queue, sales_school_outreach_executive) + createWorkTask(task_type system_follow_up, source school_lead_interactions) + two notification_events (lead_owner, sales_manager) + audit schedule_followup. editInteraction: require reason, re-validate interaction_type/outcome enums, update editable fields + interaction_status='edited', audit edit_interaction with previous_status + original snapshot (externalReference).
param_14.answer_source: followup_policy.json + interaction_policy.json + CANONICAL_DATA_MODEL.json (task_queues/work_tasks/notification_events) + convert-action chain patterns
param_14.data_entities: writes: work_tasks, task_queues (ensure), notification_events, school_lead_interactions (update on edit), audit_events
param_14.workflow_entities: schedule_followup (effect), edit_interaction (action). No lead lifecycle transition.
param_14.security_implications: Edit requires reason (P1.8) and writes an append-only audit with the original snapshot (P2.11/P3.5). interaction_type/outcome re-validated on edit (P2.2). staff_owner_id/interaction_code/interaction_status not client-editable (P3.9). The FR-0004 A01 parent-scope gap also applies to edit -> still tracked in FR-0006 (edit route will inherit the shared gen_sub scope fix).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: recipient_resolver values 'lead_owner' and 'sales_manager' are a contract consumed by notification_ops dispatch; if a resolver is unimplemented there, that reminder simply does not dispatch (the event row is still written for later processing).
param_14.assumptions: Follow-up fires only when next_followup_at is provided. Edit is allowed from interaction_status in [recorded, edited] (archived is terminal).

principles_pass.on_add_when: P3.10/P3.11 — effect runs after apply, conditional on next_followup_at; followup_policy is binding
principles_pass.task_creation: P0.5 — reuse ensureQueue/createWorkTask + canonical enums (queue_type role_queue, task_type system_follow_up)
principles_pass.reminders: reuse notification_events insert pattern (CHAIN-002); idempotency key per resolver per interaction
principles_pass.edit_reason: P1.8 — reason required for the edit; P2.11/P3.5 — original retained in append-only audit; status -> edited
principles_pass.edit_enum_revalidation: P2.2 — interaction_type/outcome re-checked on edit; 'system' excluded
principles_pass.generalize: P0.6 — on_add chain + edit land in shared gen_sub + ModuleTable detail panel

functional: add WITH next_followup_at -> creates a work_task (system_follow_up) and two notification_events
add WITHOUT next_followup_at -> no task, no notifications
ensureQueue reused when the CRM_FOLLOWUP queue already exists
edit changes editable fields + sets interaction_status='edited'
edit writes an audit event with the reason + previous status
edit of a non-existent interaction -> not found
security_owasp: A03: edit with out-of-enum interaction_type/outcome -> rejected
A08: edit cannot change staff_owner_id/interaction_code (not in editable set)
A09: audit event written for every edit (non-repudiation)
concurrency: two parallel follow-up adds -> distinct task_code + distinct notification idempotency keys
two concurrent edits of one interaction both audited (last-write-wins on the row, both recorded in audit)
